2603.11331.title: Jailbreak Scaling Laws for Large Language Models: Polynomial-Exponential Crossover
2603.11331.paper_summary: Adversarial attacks can reliably steer safety-aligned large language models toward unsafe behavior. Empirically, we find that adversarial prompt-injection attacks can amplify attack success rate from the slow polynomial growth observed without injection to exponential growth with the number of inference-time samples. To explain this phenomenon, we propose a theoretical generative model of proxy language in terms of a spin-glass system operating in a replica-symmetry-breaking regime, where generations are drawn from the associated Gibbs measure and a subset of low-energy, size-biased clusters is designated unsafe. Within this framework, we analyze prompt injection-based jailbreaking. Short injected prompts correspond to a weak magnetic field aligned towards unsafe cluster centers and yield a power-law scaling of attack success rate with the number of inference-time samples, while long injected prompts, i.e., strong magnetic field, yield exponential scaling. We derive these behaviors analytically and confirm them empirically on large language models. This transition between two regimes is due to the appearance of an ordered phase in the spin chain under a strong magnetic field, which suggests that the injected jailbreak prompt enhances adversarial order in the language model.
2603.11331.paper_summary_zh: 對抗性攻擊可以可靠地將安全對齊的大型語言模型引導至不安全的行為。經驗上，我們發現對抗性提示注入攻擊可以將攻擊成功率從沒有注入時觀察到的緩慢多項式增長放大到與推理時間樣本數量的指數增長。為了解釋這一現象，我們提出了一個理論生成模型，將代理語言視為在複製對稱破缺狀態下運作的自旋玻璃系統，其中生成來自於相關的吉布斯測度，並且一組低能量、大小偏向的簇被指定為不安全。在這一框架內，我們分析了基於提示注入的越獄。短的注入提示對應於朝向不安全簇中心對齊的弱磁場，並且隨著推理時間樣本數量的增加，攻擊成功率呈現冪律縮放，而長的注入提示，即強磁場，則呈現指數縮放。我們從理論上推導這些行為，並在大型語言模型上進行了實證驗證。這兩個狀態之間的轉變是由於在強磁場下自旋鏈中出現有序相，這表明注入的越獄提示增強了語言模型中的對抗性有序性。
2603.11331.author: Indranil Halder et.al.
2603.11331.authors: Indranil Halder, Annesya Banerjee, Cengiz Pehlevan
